But that acidity definitely kicks. Hard to tell if the wine is ageable, but it does need a rich, fatty steak.
title: McIntyre Vineyards 2009 Estate Block 3 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: Cedary aromas of wood shavings, sawmill and spiced berry evolve in the glass but hold onto a woody essence. A deep, structured palate deals flavors of wild berry, clove and chocolate, while an additional layer of chocolaty oak rises up on the finish. Drink from 2018–2030.
title: Conde de Hervías & Mencos 2009 Torre del Conde de Hervías  (Rioja)

description: Fully oaked, and you can tell via the aromas of coconut, resin, vanilla and latex that sit on top of tobacco and floral berry scents. Feels tight and high in acid, with herbal-leaning red berry flavors. Fresh and basic on the finish, with lasting acidity.
title: Piattelli 2009 Grand Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Tupungato)

description: This is a variety Gerovassiliou does very well, with its aromas of pear, peach and lemon and its creamy but crisp flavors of pear, citrus and peach. The wine has weight without losing its delicate touch, and balanced acidity gives it a food-friendly edge. A great summer white wine.
title: Gerovassiliou 2009 Malagousia (Epanomi)

description: Castello di Bolgheri enjoys some of the most coveted vineyard sites that are located a stone's throw away from the Bolgheri castle tower. The blend is Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot, and it delivers black fruit aromas, backed by wet earth, button mushroom and leather notes. It feels plush, yet structured.
title: Castello di Bolgheri 2009  Bolgheri Superiore

description: A big and dense wine, with tannins that are well integrated into its core of solid black plum and cherry flavors, and a bitter chocolate note that comes from wood aging. With all of its concentration, this wine should age for five years.
title: Château de Tiregand 2009  Pécharmant

description: Still firmly tannic, this impressive wine is still developing. Made from old vines that give minerality and concentration, it is very dense allowing some juicy black plum flavors to show through. With its dry tannins, it's going to need more aging. Drink from 2017.
title: Lavradores de Feitoria 2009 Três Bagos Grande Escolha Red (Douro)

description: Surprising density and smoothness characterizes this structured Pinot Grigio. The wine is redolent of peach, melon and mature grapefruit with a touch of nutty almond on the close.
title: Marco Felluga 2009 Mongris Pinot Grigio (Collio)

description: Appley and fresh with hints of white peach and blossoms on the nose and palate, this is a straightforward yet well-made Pinot Grigio that's hard to resist for under $10. Snappy and clean with brisk acidity and a pretty green floral finish.
title: Cramele Recas 2009 Dreambird Pinot Grigio (Viile Timisului)

description: This is ripe and jammy, with soft fruit and attractive acidity. It is warm, with a hint of wood aging. Ready to drink.
title: L. Tramier & Fils 2009 Premier Cru  (Beaune)

description: This is golden-tan in color. The nose is gaseous and smells of ham and yeast. The palate is distinctly foamy, while flavors of briny apple finish mild yet heavy.
designation: Blanca Cusine Gran Reserva Brut Nature
title: Parés Baltà 2009 Blanca Cusine Gran Reserva Brut Nature Sparkling (Cava)

description: A beautiful Sauvignon Blanc, dry and crisp and low in alcohol. With no oak to interfere, it shows vibrant citrus fruit, guava, lemongrass and mineral flavors, balanced with zesty acidity. A beautiful, racy wine.
title: Voss 2009 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)

description: Bright acidity gives the tangerine and citron flavors of this Chardonnay a bite. It's very dry, clean and minerally, and finishes with a mouthwatering scour.
title: Schug 2009 Chardonnay (Carneros)

description: Here's a good, rich Cabernet for drinking now. It satisfies for the elaborate layers of blackberries, cherries and plums, enhanced with spicy, smoky oak. Full-bodied and tannic, it shows lots of elegant complexity for the price.
title: Martin Ray 2009 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa-Mendocino-Sonoma)

description: Ripe and fruity, this bears its red berry flavor right up front. It has richness and concentration, and the tannins give a core of dryness to this otherwise fruit-forward wine.
title: Château Gamage 2009  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: New wood dominates this wine, which is full of rich tannins, and notes of smoky wood, licorice and black coffee. The fruit feels extracted, hard-edged and too strong for its own good.
title: Château Péby Faugères 2009  Saint-Émilion

description: Dry and acidic, with interesting flavors of spices, wild forest red berries, cola and bacon. An exotic, low alcohol Pinot Noir that seems at its best now and for a couple years.
title: Kutch 2009 Falstaff Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: A lovely Pinot Noir, dry and silky, giving lots of pleasure in the mouth. Shows polished cherry, raspberry and spice flavors. From Cargasacchi.
title: Point Concepción 2009 Salsipuedes Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)

description: Medium bodied and firmly structured, this doesn't show the generosity of the 2009 vintage as much as some other Nothern Rhônes. Instead, it's a traditionally built wine with classic notes of smoke, herbs and black olives, and even some ash-like notes on the finish. Drink 2013–2018.
title: Domaine Les Hauts de Mercurol 2009  Crozes-Hermitage

description: Intensely ripe wine, with melon and pear flavors, spiced with just the right amount of wood and structured fruit skin tannins. So classy, this is a beautiful wine, boasting the richness of 2009 while still keeping a sense of nervy texture.
title: Louis Latour 2009 Les Demoiselles  (Chevalier-Montrachet)

description: The color of this very sweet wine is deep amber. Aromas are savory and rich, while flavors are unctuous and nutty. It's fun to drink, and tastes like a mature wine.
title: Stonehedge 2009 Chardonnay (Mendocino County)

description: The vines from which the fruit for this wine were sourced are, on average, 15 years old. Aged primarily in French oak, this concentrated wine shows fine depth, with an emphasis on black cherry and cassis flavors. Full and ripe, this well-structured effort has the balance and polished tannins to be cellared for a decade, at least.
title: Northstar 2009 Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This shows bold oak tones of toast and spice that will unwind with 3–5 more years of bottle age. Beyond the notes of cinnamon and tobacco, this blend of Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc shows austerity and complexity, with touches of dark plum and tangy barbecue sauce. Give it a few more years.
title: Tenuta I Greppi 2009 Greppicaia  (Bolgheri Superiore)

description: Light and fruity, with young vine flavors of strawberries and cherries.
title: O•S Winery 2009 Sheridan Vineyard Cabernet Franc (Yakima Valley)

description: Pérez Cruz makes some nice wines, but this blend of Petit Verdot, Carmenère and Malbec is so full of herbaceous aromas and flavors of juniper, olive and coffee grinds that it's a struggle to get through. Yes, the mouthfeel and structure are that of a serious wine. But the aromas and flavors are so green and mean that it's impossible to rate this blend higher.
title: Viña Pérez Cruz 2009 Quelen Special Selection Red (Maipo Valley)

description: The Gonet family's investment, which began in 1998, has begun to bear fruit. This wine comes from the second in a row of fine Bordeaux vintages. The style of the vintage is well represented in this richly endowed wine with its swathes of black fruits, its rich tannins and its warm full texture. This is just coming together after a quiet period for the vintage. Drink from 2018.
title: Château Haut-Bacalan 2009  Pessac-Léognan

description: This is a soft, easy wine, with sweet black raspberry, cherry and spice flavors. Drink now with a nice homemade cheeseburger.
title: Curtis 2009 Syrah (Santa Barbara County)

description: A bit lean and tannic, with an herbal tang to the tart cherry and cranberry fruit. This doesn't show much of the warmth of the vintage but remains a pleasant wine, best served alongside fatty foods like burgers.
title: Louis Bernard 2009 Domaine Le Garrigon Red (Côtes du Rhône)

description: This is a new blend from Baer. Merlot dominant, it's forward, fruity and fresh, offering pleasant strawberry and mocha flavors. Though at first sip it seems to fade quickly, it becomes more fleshy in the glass and thickens up nicely.
title: Baer 2009 Star Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This beautiful blend of Cabernet Franc, Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon opens with bold intensity and personality. Yes, this is a plush and modern wine, but it is also unforgettably delicious. Bright cherry and blackberry are backed by chocolate, spice, leather and pipe tobacco.
title: Tenuta di Biserno 2009 Biserno Red (Toscana)

description: Apple and pear flavors dominate this attractive wine. It has a simple, fruit-forward character, and it's soft, rounded and developing well. On the finish, a more tangy, fresh character shows.
title: Domaine Viticole de la Ville de Colmar 2009 Florimont Grand Cru Riesling (Alsace)
winery: Domaine Viticole de la Ville de Colmar

description: Ripe and fruity, this has sweet black-currant and plum fruits, a layer of rounded tannins and a juicy, full-in-the-mouth character. It will be ready to drink in a year.
title: Château Tour du Moulin 2009 Les Terres Rouges  (Fronsac)

description: A hugely rich, powerful Chardonnay, layering ripe fruit with a core of tannic structure, acidity, wood and huge depth of flavor. The wine is concentrated, with layers of fruit and textured acidity. Of course, it needs aging—7 years at least.
title: Louis Jadot 2009  Corton-Charlemagne

description: El Bosque is Eguren family top-shelf Rioja that's a knockout. It's dark as night, with dusty aromas of cola, cocoa, marzipan, blackberry and spice all rolled into a finessed whole. Feels tight as a drum but balanced, with monstrous flavors of boysenberry, cassis, peppercorn, baking spices and chocolate. Young and raring to go; hold until 2013 then drink through 2018.
title: Sierra Cantabria 2009 Finca El Bosque  (Rioja)

description: The entire flight of Prinz Salm's 2009 GG was something of a letdown, including this astronomically priced sample. It's good, but nothing more, combining subtle apple and citrus notes with hints of petrol. Feels lean and crisp despite the 13.5% abv, so perhaps it just needs more time to develop.
title: Prinz Salm 2009 Wallhausen Felseneck GG Trocken Riesling (Nahe)

description: Solid but generic berry fruit aromas are clean and come with herbal, minty edging. There's no shortage of acidity, so the palate has a sharp, crisp feel and tart, oaky flavors of berry and plum. Sheer, hard, tart and dry on the finish.
title: TerraNoble 2009 Reserva Syrah (Colchagua Valley)

description: Smells floral and foresty, with solid plum and cherry scents offset by rubber. Feels fresh and zesty, with semisharp acidity pushing bright flavors of raspberry, red plum and dry oak. Turns a touch more oaky late, as vanilla and coconut flavors rise up. From five grapes led by Carmenère.
title: Chono 2009 San Lorenzo Estate Red (Maipo Valley)

description: Spicy, new wood aromas dominate this firm and structured wine. It has weight and a density that comes both from the wood and ripe fruits. Big and solid, it has a fine red berry aftertaste.
title: Mas de la Rouvière 2009 Red (Bandol)

description: Earthy plum and berry aromas dominate a fruit-forward bouquet. A layered, proper feeling palate delivers lightly buttered flavors of dark plum, creamy oak and spice. On the finish, this tastes a little toasty and peppery, while the feel is comported.
title: Faustino Rivero Ulecia 2009 Yellow Label Crianza  (Rioja)

description: A bit of an anomaly for this fine Ruwer estate, where being merely good is a disappointment. It starts off with attractive stone and ripe apple notes, but seems somewhat  acetic on the palate—there's an intrusive pickle-like note.
title: Maximin Grünhäuser 2009 Abtsberg Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

description: Touriga Nacional and Cabernet Sauvignon merge seamlessly in this richly structured but fruity wine. It is densely concentrated, with a line of tannins cutting through the blackberry fruit flavors. Firm, textured and likely to age well, this is impressive.
title: Quinta do Casal Monteiro 2009 Forma de Arte Reserva Red (Tejo)

description: This fragrant red opens with scents suggesting aromatic Alpine herb, ripe dark-skinned berry, flint and a whiff of exotic spice. The luminous, elegantly structured palate displays ripe wild cherry, raspberry, mineral and a hint of star anise alongside a backbone of firm, refined tannins. Drink through 2024.
title: Nera 2009 Inferno Riserva  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: Too sweet, like pineapple, orange and lemon soda with a squeeze of vanilla. Has some nice acidity, though.
title: TV Celadon 2009 Grenache Blanc (Arroyo Seco)

description: If you're used to soft, ripe Napa Valley Cabernet, this one, grown just over the Lake County border, will strike you as dry and unyielding. The tannins are pronounced, giving it a hard bite. But it's rich and deep in crème de cassis, the sort of wine that seems ageworthy. Take a gamble and put it in your cellar for 6–8 years.
title: Obsidian Ridge 2009 Obsidian Ridge Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Red Hills Lake County)

description: Packed tight with power and minty, earthy berry aromas that come with a dry dustiness. Feels ripe and proper, with a slight tannic grab and full but fresh flavors of wild berry, cassis, cocoa and oak. On the finish, chocolate and dry spice flavors take over, yielding a pleasant, complete whole. Drink now through 2015.
title: Finca La Mata 2009  Ribera del Duero

description: Defines Russian River Valley Pinot Noir in the dryness, silkiness and full-bodied richness, with flavors of cherries, pomegranates, raspberries and slightly underripe persimmons. This complex wine grows better as it warms in the glass. Drink over the next 2–3 years.
title: Bailiwick 2009 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Powerful and structured, this offers both the weight and the potential for rich fruit. Dominated at this stage by tannins and wood-influenced flavors, the black plum note is likely to come through in the next year. The acidity is crisp, and a bitter cherry character flavors the finish.
title: Château Trocard Monrepos 2009  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: Inzolia is a fantastic partner to raw tuna, shrimp or ceviche. This example is simple and clean, with bright citrus, peach, lime and white flower.
title: D'Alessandro 2009 Inzolia (Sicilia)

description: Peppery and trending toward stemmy flavors, this has a phenolic bite. The brambly berry fruit runs quickly into a slightly warm finish.
title: àMaurice 2009 Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: They say red Vinho Verde is an acquired taste, and with its very tight tannins and acidity, this wine fits the billing. Think of it with sardines or fatty meats.
title: Campelo 2009 Tinto Red (Vinho Verde)

description: Okay for everyday drinking, with crisp acidity and a clean, steely minerality undergirding the lime flavors. The drawback is a strong feline smell and taste that some will find offensive.
title: Pedroncelli 2009 East Side Vineyards Sauvignon Blanc (Dry Creek Valley)

description: Filled with aromas and flavors of passion fruit, gooseberries and a touch of grassiness, this is a readily accessible Sauvignon Blanc that might lack a little complexity and richness, but is assertively flavored and goes down easily.
title: Saint Clair 2009 Vicar's Choice Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: A straightforward, slightly chunky Côtes du Rhône, with modest cherry fruit tinged with leather, chocolate and licorice. It's medium to full in body, with acids that stick out a bit on the palate, but which should serve it well when paired with burgers or meatloaf.
title: Louis Bernard 2009 Red (Côtes du Rhône)

description: Plump and round on the palate, this is a bit soft and lacking bite, but does offer a reasonably complex array of pungently herbal notes and melon and fig flavors. Drink now.
title: Twin Islands 2009 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: This Dolcetto offers a correct nose with fresh layers of cherry, blueberry and wild berries. There isn't huge intensity here but the wine does offer a full, soft mouthfeel with some mild tannins on the close.
title: Cantine San Silvestro 2009 Primaio  (Dolcetto d'Alba)

description: Part of a range of young wines produced by Mas del Perié. This is bright and fruity, relying on black berry fruits, soft tannins and a vivid juicy character. The dusty tannins supply a light amount of structure.
title: Lannac Saint-Jean 2009 Le Chemin Malbec (Cahors)

description: A very oaky style of Malbec. The bouquet is full of cough drop, milk chocolate and mint, while the palate feels solid and beefy, but resiny. Flavors of berry fruits are softened and sweetened by oak and chocolate, while the finish is woody and tannic.
title: Altocedro 2009 Gran Reserva Malbec (La Consulta)

description: Including nearly a quarter Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Malbec, this estate grown Cabernet Franc is a bit aromatically muted but brings juicy cherry and butterscotch flavors and soft, plush tannins.
title: RoxyAnn 2009 Cabernet Franc (Rogue Valley)

description: Cool-climate flavors, just barely ripe, create a light wine overloaded with herb and stem flavors. The fruit is thin and pale, rhubarb and cranberry.
title: Montinore 2009 Estate Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Designed as a paring match to game foods and wild turkey, La Cattura is 85% Teroldego (a native variety of northern Italy) and 15% Syrah. This specific blend results in raw, milky tones of creamy blueberry and mocha.
title: Poggio al Casone 2009 La Cattura Red (Toscana)

description: Opens with scratchy, dusty aromas of red fruits and herbs. The palate is round but clunky, with sweet, grapy flavors of blackberry and blueberry. Finishes rather oaky and mild, with a grainy feel.
title: Vistalba 2009 Corte C Malbec-Cabernet Sauvignon (Mendoza)

description: Spicy and raw, this features tight berry aromas, followed by licorice and rum cake accents. A blast of heat and ripe fruit appears on the finish.
title: Tenuta Fujanera 2009 Arrocco Nero di Troia (Puglia)

description: This is a forward, even plush Reserve, loaded with generous black fruits and streaked with anise and cola notes. At first, the wine seemed to fade a bit too quickly, robbing the finish of expected complexity. But retasted on the second day, the wine had fleshed out beautifully.
title: Chehalem 2009 Reserve Pinot Noir (Ribbon Ridge)

description: Aromas of scorched earth, prune and dark cooking spice lead the nose while the mature palate shows raisin, dried berry, leather and a hint of anise. Smooth but fleeting tannins indicate this has seen better days. Drink now.
title: Villa Montignana 2009 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: A very dry, crisp white wine with a neutral color and equally neutral aromas and flavors. It's lean, green apple-driven and fresh, but not what you'd call loaded with personality. Cleanses the palate and washes things down, but does so quietly.
title: Elki 2009 Vinas Viejas Pedro Ximénez (Elqui Valley)

description: A blend of Grenache, Shiraz and Mourvèdre, this is much more firmly tannic and structured than most such Australian blends. Grilled meat, red plum, black tea and rose hip notes are crisp and firm on the palate. Drink 2015–2023, and possibly beyond that.
title: D'Arenberg 2009 The Ironstone Pressings Red (McLaren Vale)

description: Nicely dry and acidic, giving a clean, scoury mouthfeel to this food-friendly wine. The flavors combine tart citrus fruits with vanilla, yeast, minerals and peppery spices. A crisp cocktail wine to start the meal.
title: Tessa Marie 2009 Vermentino (Santa Barbara County)

description: Here's a stylish, flashy Napa red that's ready to drink now, even without decanting. It's rich and layered with blackberries, cherries and cassis, folded into sweet, soft, complex tannins. It's a Cabernet Sauvignon-based Bordeaux blend with 14% Sangiovese, which seems to add acidity.
title: Pure Cru 2009 PureCoz Red (Napa Valley)

description: This is a very nice Cabernet that's gentle and soft in the mouth, with luscious blackberry and mocha flavors. It seems a little too light for aging, but should make for excellent drinking now and over the next year or two.
title: Schug 2009 Cabernet Sauvignon (Sonoma Valley)

description: This shows beautiful aromatic intensity, with plush ripeness and sweet tones of wild berry and spice. There's a blast of moist tobacco on the close followed by tight tannins and fine structure.
title: Marchesi di Barolo 2009 Serragrilli  (Barbaresco)

description: Very fresh, fine and elegant, this has savory aromas of cured meat, rusty earth and white truffle followed by notes of dried fruit and currant.
title: Mauro Veglio 2009 Angelo Nebbiolo (Langhe)

description: Also known as a Cape Blend, this mix of 46% Pinotage, 30% Cabernet Sauvignon, 18% Merlot and 6% Cabernet Franc is an easy-drinking, youthful wine with fresh but rich fruit flavors of red plum and cherry backed by rose hip and hibiscus floral accents. Structured tannins remain through the close, with a subtle smokiness unfolding on the end.
title: Kanonkop 2009 Kadette Red (Stellenbosch)

description: Marked by deep, delicious flavors of chocolate, blueberries and blackberries, this is a lovely Cabernet to drink now. The tannins are firm, the finish long and spicy. Shows real Napa class.
title: Raymond 2009 Reserve Selection Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Fine in acidity and elegantly dry, this is a glorious Cabernet, marked with intensely ripe flavors of blackberry and black currant. Delicious and ripe, it's sturdy in tannins. Really needs some time to come around. Best from 2015–2021 or so.
title: Stonestreet 2009 Rockfall Cabernet Sauvignon (Alexander Valley)

description: Gen Yers and Baby Boomers will remember when Rosemount's ubiquitous Diamond Label wines represented great value. This Cab offers slightly confected cherry fruit, a whisper of mint and hints of vanilla. It's round in the mouth without being heavy, a fresh fruity wine for immediate gratification.
title: Rosemount 2009 Diamond Label Cabernet Sauvignon (South Eastern Australia)

description: Opens with hard, rubbery, leathery aromas. In the mouth, it's thick and chewy with black fruit but also an herbal edge. There's depth and plenty of oak here, and it finishes sweet and relatively smooth. Starts rough, improves in the middle, and then finishes fairly well.
title: Rutini 2009 Trumpeter Malbec-Syrah (Mendoza)

description: Warm and juicy, this has a typical rustic edge. It's packed with plum and dark prune flavors, with a rounded and soft mouthfeel that's layered with attractive acidity. Glass stopper.
title: Gernot and Heike Heinrich 2009 St. Laurent (Burgenland)

description: There are good, dry flavors in this Cabernet. It's a little on the coarse side, but shows lots of extraction to the blackberries and black currants. The tannins are hard and firm, and not likely to age out, so drink up.
title: Babcock 2009 The Loin Cabernet Sauvignon (Santa Ynez Valley)
